steps.title: sin X = cos Y means X + Y = 90°
steps.content: $\frac{\sin X}{\cos Y} = 1$ → $\sin X = \cos Y$. The complementary identity says: if $\sin A = \cos B$ (both acute), then $A + B = 90°$.

steps.title: Plug in Y = 32° and solve
steps.content: $X + 32° = 90°$ → $X = 58°$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
